summary: Large plbonuslib matching PR that moved many player bonus/stat functions and prototypes into plbonuslib, expanded player bonus/stale-move data structures, and corrected cross-system function signatures needed by the match. The main payload was about 1000 added lines in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c plus 80+ public prototypes in plbonuslib.h. It also cleaned up includes from pl_0371.h to plbonuslib.h, introduced pl/inlines.h with pl_CheckIfSameTeam, and refined types in pl/types.h, ft/types.h, player.h, plbonus.h, gm_1601.h, ftlib, and ft_0877/ft_0892. Review focused on include-path correctness: relative includes in headers can resolve relative to the compiling .c file rather than the header, so headers should use fully qualified project includes.
what_changed: Matched a substantial portion of src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c, growing the file by roughly 1000 lines and adding many pl_8003xxxx/pl_80040xxx bonus-stat functions.
Expanded src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h from a small partial header to a broad public interface for plbonuslib functions from 0x03D60C through 0x040DB8.
Removed many plbonuslib-related declarations from src/melee/pl/pl_0371.h and updated many fighter/item users to include src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h instead.
Added src/melee/pl/inlines.h with static inline bool pl_CheckIfSameTeam(int slotA, int slotB), using gm_8016B168 and Player_GetTeam.
Added and refined player bonus/stat structs in src/melee/pl/types.h, including pl_800386D8_t, StaleMoveTable, pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, and pl_804D6470_t.
Changed Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr to return &player->stale_moves.total_attack_count_struct instead of pointer-casting from total_attack_count.
Changed Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2 to return pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t* and cast from &player->stale_moves, reflecting an extended view over the stale move table storage.
Typed global pl_804D6470 as pl_804D6470_t* rather than void* and changed pl_80038914 to return pl_804D6470_t*.

Corrected function signatures used by matching, including Player_GetTeam returning int, pl_800386D8 returning unsigned int, ftLib_8008777C returning float and returning atan2f, ft_80087AA4 returning s32, ft_800898B4 returning ft_800898B4_t*, and selected gm_1601 prototypes gaining concrete parameters/returns.
Refined Fighter damage layout by replacing separate x18d8/x18dc/kb_applied1 fields with an ft_800898B4_t x18d8 substruct and updating uses such as fp->dmg.x18d8.kb_applied1.
Refined Fighter union Struct2070 bitfield layout by representing x2072 both as grouped nibbles and individual bits.
smart_moves: Separated the newly matched plbonuslib interface into plbonuslib.h and migrated users away from pl_0371.h, reducing header ambiguity around the 0x03Dxxx-0x040xxx player bonus functions.

Introduced typed overlay structs such as pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t and ft_800898B4_t instead of continuing raw byte-offset casts; this made code like temp_r31_2->xDD1.bit6 and fp->dmg.x18d8.kb_applied1 more explicit.
Kept uncertain layouts evidence-grounded with offset fields, UNKNOWN SIZE comments, and static assertions where size was known, especially STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct StaleMoveTable) == 0xCF4).
Used an inline helper for repeated team-check logic rather than duplicating gm_8016B168/Player_GetTeam comparisons inside plbonuslib functions.
Adjusted return types at call boundaries to satisfy matching and semantics, e.g. explicitly comparing pl_800386D8(...) != 0 in ftFx_AppealS after changing its return type to unsigned int.
Replaced placeholder UNK_T/UNK_RET or void signatures with more concrete signatures where the diff showed usage evidence.
decomp_lessons: When matching a library that reads deep player statistics, invest in struct overlays for repeated offset ranges rather than scattering casts to UnkFlagStruct or byte pointers.
A partially known struct can still be useful if it has correct offsets, known member types, and static size checks for the known base portion.
Public header migration is part of matching: once functions are owned by a more specific module like plbonuslib, update include users and remove stale declarations from catch-all headers.
Changing a function return type can require small caller-side expression changes to preserve codegen, such as adding an explicit != 0 comparison.
Header include style matters for this project’s build/compiler behavior; relative includes in headers can resolve incorrectly depending on the .c file being compiled.
Use fully qualified includes in headers when there is any chance the header will be included from another subsystem.
naming_conventions: New unknown-but-typed structs used address/function-derived names, e.g. ft_800898B4_t, pl_800386D8_t, pl_804D6470_t, and pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t.
Extended stale-move storage was represented as pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t with a first member named x0_staleMoveTable to indicate an embedded base StaleMoveTable at offset 0.
Offset-based field names such as xD54, xDD1, x674, x710, x18d8, and kb_applied1 were retained where semantics were incomplete.
Bitfields in unknown flags were named bit0 through bit7 inside union wrappers such as xDD0 and xDD1.
Function names remained address-based pl_8003xxxx/pl_80040xxx while prototypes gained more accurate argument and return types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used RETURN_IF-style early exits to express control flow in matched plbonuslib functions.
Converted do/while style iteration into for loops over the six player slots where codegen allowed it; the author left a TODO noting many hardcoded 6s may correspond to Gm_Player_NumMax.
Added exact offset structs and arrays for stale-move/item-log counters, enabling direct field accesses like x674[pl_itemlog_kind] and x710[pl_itemlog_kind].
Added HSD_ASSERTMSG checks around item-log-kind bounds in pl_8003E2CC/pl_8003E334/pl_8003E39C/pl_8003E420 according to the diff evidence.
Corrected ftLib_8008777C to return the atan2f result, which both fixes the signature and makes the function body semantically consistent.
Reworked Fighter damage substructure access so ft_800898B4 returns a typed pointer to GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->dmg.x18d8.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: The compiler treats relative includes as relative to the .c file being compiled, not necessarily the header. A header including "forward.h" could accidentally include melee/ft/forward.h when pulled in from melee/ft/fighter.c. Suggested fully qualifying as <melee/pl/forward.h>.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: This is a real breakage risk when a header is included from a non-pl header. Relative includes are fine in C files, but not in headers.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The project is probably going to phase out relative includes anyway, referencing #1565.

review_feedback.author: Avery-Brinkman
review_feedback.feedback: Author summarized the lesson as avoiding relative includes in headers because they can cause problems; reviewer clarified that relative includes in C files are acceptable for now.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace relative includes in headers with fully qualified includes after #1565.
Look for follow-up PRs that rename pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t fields or identify pl_804D6470_t semantics.
Search for later changes to hardcoded player slot count 6 in plbonuslib and whether it became Gm_Player_NumMax.
Check subsequent matches in plbonuslib around pl_8003E4A4, pl_8003EA40, pl_8003ED0C, and pl_80040xxx functions.
Find later PRs that consolidate pl_0371.h declarations into more specific pl headers.
